debug_info.goal:
d : ℕ
i : Fin d
μ : Fin d → Measure ℝ
inst✝ : ∀ (i : Fin d), IsProbabilityMeasure (μ i)
f : ℝ → ℝ
hf : Integrable f (μ i)
⊢ Integrable (fun a => f (a i)) (Measure.pi μ)

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
have h₁ : Integrable (fun a : (Fin d → ℝ) => f (a i)) (Measure.pi μ) := by
    -- Use the fact that the product of probability measures has the Fubini property
    have h₂ : Fubini (Measure.pi μ) := by
      apply Fubini.pi
      intro i
      exact by
        -- Each measure μ i is a probability measure, hence σ-finite
        have h₃ : IsProbabilityMeasure (μ i) := inferInstance
        exact h₃.sigmaFinite
    -- Use the Fubini theorem to reduce the product space to individual spaces
    -- and apply the given integrability of f with respect to μ i
    refine' h₂.integral_congr_ae _ _ hf
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Finset.prod_const]) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try
      {
        refine' (fun a => f (a i))
      })
  exact h₁
